name: Paupiettes De Boeuf
recipeIngredient: 1 Pound Bottom round of beef,  see note
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut beef into 16 slices about 4 inches square.
2. In a heavy skillet over medium-low heat, melt 2 tablespoons of butter and sauté the onion and mushrooms until the onion is translucent.
3. Stir in the lemon rind, bread crumbs, parsley, 1 teaspoon salt, and 1/4 teaspoon pepper.
4. When the parsley has wilted (about 1 minute after adding it to the skillet), quickly stir in the eggs to bind the mixture and remove the skillet from heat at once.
5. Set aside.
6. Flatten each beef piece with a rolling pin or wooden mallet until very thin and about twice its original size.
7. Season each piece with a little salt, pepper, and a pinch of thyme as you finish them.
8. At the widest end of each beef slice, place 1 teaspoon of bread-crumb mixture from the skillet.
9. Roll up the meat into a sausage shape and secure it with a wooden toothpick through the center.
10. Roll each piece in flour.
11. In a very large skillet over medium-high heat, melt 4 tablespoons of butter and brown the pieces of beef.
12. As you finish cooking them, place them in the slow cooker.
13. Pour water into the skillet, scrape up the pan juices, and turn the sauce into the cooker.
14. Cover and cook on Low for about 5 hours.
15. About half an hour before serving, mix the garlic and mustard into the sauce around the beef; cover; turn the heat to High and cook for 30 minutes.
16. If the sauce seems less flavorful than desired, add a dab of prepared mustard and a little salt about 5 minutes before serving.
17. If the sauce seems thin, leave the cover off during the second cooking period.
